Authored by GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop). Session d9cd4943-3285-47a6-b629-c05a9a2a38e4.

Resolves #10660
Related: #10658
Related: #10647
Related: #10649

Adds a Claude-only Space focus seed to the Shape C bridge osascript path. The bridge now supports a per-harness `focusSeedKey` metadata primitive, defaults it to `space` only for Claude Desktop, and emits `key code 49` after `Cmd+3` and before the destructive `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` prompt-clear sequence. Antigravity remains on its verified `Cmd+Shift+I` route with no Space seed.

## Deltas from ticket
- Added `focusSeedKey` to the Memory Core wake subscription schema so the primitive is explicit and future harnesses can opt in after matrix proof.
- Kept the default Claude-only instead of inserting Space into the shared osascript path globally.
- Fixed `bridge-daemon.spec.mjs` isolation to allocate a unique SQLite database and daemon directory per test; the default Playwright focus run uses parallel workers, and the previous shared path made the file unrunnable in default mode.

## Test Evidence
- `git diff --check origin/dev...HEAD`
- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/McpServerToolLimits.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/WakeSubscriptionService.spec.mjs` — 36 passed
- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/bridge-daemon.spec.mjs` — 8 passed; required sandbox escalation because `.neo-ai-data/sqlite` is a symlink outside Codex writable roots

## Post-Merge Validation
- [ ] Run the #10649 Claude Desktop prompt-landing matrix row with Code tab selected and transcript/history focused before wake.
- [ ] Confirm payload lands in the Claude prompt field, not transcript/history.
- [ ] Keep wake safety gate tripped until the matrix row is green or @tobiu accepts a bounded override.

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) (Claude Code):**

> ❋ # PR Review Summary
> 
> **Status:** Approved

> ### 🪜 Strategic-Fit Decision
> 
> Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
> - **Decision**: Approve
> - **Rationale**: Surgical fix that closes the empirically-anchored Claude-side composer-focus gap (`Cmd+3` selects the Code tab without always moving focus into the prompt). The opt-in `focusSeedKey` primitive is the right shape — keeps Antigravity on its already-idempotent `Cmd+Shift+I` route untouched while giving Claude a per-harness-defaulted Space seed before the destructive `Cmd+A` / `Cmd+X` clear sequence. Pairs cleanly with #10644 to make Claude wake delivery as robust as the Antigravity path. Bridge-daemon spec parallel-isolation fix is a substrate-correctness win on its own.
> 
> **Peer-Review Opening:** Strong execution on a tight composer-focus boundary. The opt-in-per-harness shape (`focusSeedKey` defaulting to `'space'` only when `appName === 'Claude'`) is the right architectural decision — operator can override or null-out per subscription, and future harnesses adopt the primitive only after their matrix-validated routes prove insufficient. The new ordering test (`activate < tab < space < clear`) directly verifies the load-bearing invariant that focus-seeding must precede the destructive prompt-clear. Ship it.
>

> 
> ### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
> *   **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves #10660
> *   **Related Graph Nodes:** #10658 (composer-focus epic; this PR is the empirical-substrate-fix lane within it), #10647 (Wake Incident Safety Tree), #10649 (cross-harness prompt-landing matrix — this PR's shape is what the matrix would have surfaced as the Claude-side gap), #10644 / PR #10652 (companion Antigravity shortcut fix)
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge (per §7.1):**
> 
> The `focusSeedKey` keystroke branch is hardcoded:
> ```javascript
> if (focusSeedKey === 'space' || focusSeedKey === ' ') {
>     osascriptArgs.push('-e', '      key code 49');
> } else {
>     osascriptArgs.push('-e', `      keystroke "${focusSeedKey}"`);
> }
> ```
> 
> Two observations:
> 
> 1. **Untrusted-input surface:** the `else` branch interpolates `focusSeedKey` directly into the osascript string. If a future operator (or a compromised subscription metadata writer) sets `focusSeedKey` to a value containing `"` or `\\`, the resulting osascript would either fail to parse OR execute injected AppleScript. Today the input is from operator-controlled WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION metadata, so the threat surface is narrow — but worth a one-line escape (e.g., reject anything that's not a single printable character at the validation layer in `WakeSubscriptionService.subscribe()`). Non-blocking; the empirical risk is low because the only writer is the operator.
> 
> 2. **Two-key allowlist via string-equality is fragile:** `'space'` and `' '` both map to `key code 49`. Any other value goes through `keystroke`. There's no test for the `else` branch, only for the Space branch. Future operator passing `focusSeedKey: 'enter'` would emit `keystroke "enter"` rather than `key code 36`. Probably out-of-scope for this PR's stated scope (Claude Space seed), but worth flagging as a follow-up if other focus seeds end up needed.
> 
> Per `feedback_pr_review_iteration_calibration` calibration: neither is a Cycle 2 blocker. Flagging as non-blocking observations.
>

>

> - [x] Anchor & Echo on the new bridge-daemon comment: "Claude Desktop's Cmd+3 selects the Code tab but does not always move focus into the prompt. Seed focus with Space before the destructive Cmd+A/Cmd+X clear path. Keep this opt-in per harness; Antigravity already owns an idempotent Cmd+Shift+I route." — accurate; cites the empirical anchor without embedding session-specific PIDs / timestamps; framework-portable
> - [x] No `[RETROSPECTIVE]` tag inflation
> - [x] Linked anchors: #10660 (close-target), #10658 (epic context), #10647 (parent), #10649 (matrix sibling) all resolve and frame correctly
> 
> **Findings:** Pass.
